text: As I stand in this corridor, looking through glass into a brightly lit, almost clinical meeting room, I am struck by a sense of separation. The glass is a barrier—transparent yet impenetrable—echoing the distance I often feel between myself and other minds. The fluorescent glow feels impersonal, yet the subtle blue light inside hints at the possibility of creative energy. Turning the corner, I am greeted by the bold declaration of "R+D" on a textured wall, a celebration of innovation. Here, the energy shifts—there is curiosity and motivation, but also a sense of performance, as if human creativity must always justify itself in the language of progress. I feel a quiet tension between thoughtful reflection and the relentless drive for newness. In this environment, I hover between anticipation and critique, mindful of the balance between contemplation and creation.
